content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

SACRAMENTO                                650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2630
                                         SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
770 L STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814             TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800                FILE NUMBER
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752
                                                                                  6437.02


                             January 10, 1990


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Chief, Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

     Re:  Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice

Dear Jeanne:

     Recently, I telephoned your office to ask your opinion as to
whether a proposed initiative submitted to the Attorney General
and given an official summary date, but never printed or
circulated for signature purposes, would trigger a 21-day post
petition turn-in campaign statement pursuant to Government Code
§84200.5(f).  You indicated that no such filing would be
required based on the following set of facts.

     Our client, Gary J. Flynn, submitted a proposed initiative
to the Attorney General on May 26, 1989.  The Attorney General
identified the proposed initiative as #SA-89RF0013.  The Attorney
General provided a title and summary of the initiative and
designated the official summary date as July 19, 1989.  Prior to
July 18, 1989, our client wished to make technical amendments to
the proposed initiative.  These technical amendments had not been
submitted to the Attorney General prior to the Attorney General
finalizing title and summary on July 18, 1989.  In our
conversations with the Attorney General's office, it was their
recommendation that even though the amendments were technical in
nature, they would treat the amended initiative as a new
initiative and start the title and summary period over again.
The Attorney General's office, however, did not expedite the
title and summary since they recognized these amendments to be
technical.  On July 31, 1989, we submitted to the Attorney
General's office the revised initiative.  On August 2, 1989, the
Attorney General provided title and summary for the initiative
and designated August 2, 1989 as the official summary date.  This
initiative was numbered by the Attorney General as SA-89RE0018.


If there is supposed to be text in this document, it may be:
- Too faint to read clearly
- Not visible in this image quality
- Present in a format that hasn't rendered properly

Could you please provide a clearer version of the document or verify that the correct image was uploaded?

Jeanne Pritchard
January 10, 1990
Page 2


     The amended initiative was printed and circulated to voters
for signature. On December 5, 1989, the first petitions of the
initiative were turned in to several county clerks. Pursuant to
Government Code §84200.5(f) the committee, Citizens for
Legislative Ethics, supported by individuals and Republican
organizations, which had been formed to support the qualification
of the initiative, was required to file within 21 days of
December 5, 1989. The committee did make a public disclosure on
FPPC form 420 on December 26, 1989, covering the period January 1
through December 19, 1989.

     For the first initiative that was never circulated, the
Attorney General had established December 15, 1989 as the last
day for proponents to file with the county clerks. Since this
date was subsequent to the date petitions were first submitted on
the circulated initiative, we questioned whether an additional
filing would be required by our committee, calculating 21 days
after December 15, 1989? We also told you that this committee
had neither raised or spent any money during the 10 days.

     You advised that since the amendments were technical in
nature and since the first initiative was never printed or
circulated, it would not be necessary to file a campaign
statement as required by Government Code §84200.5(f) for the
first initiative numbered SA-89RF0013.

     If I have misstated any of the facts of our conversation or
my understanding of your advice to us, please notify me at your
earliest convenience. If this letter is an accurate
memorialization of our conversation, please sign this letter
below and return it to me for our records. If you have any
questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                   Kind regards,

                                   Kirk Alan Pessner
                                   Paralegal/Administrator
                                   Political Reports Unit


KAP:bw
0108901.ltr

_____________________________
Jeanne Pritchard
